title: NOC 73113 – Floor covering installers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73113 professionals are the precision finishing experts responsible for the installation and repair of various flooring systems across Canada. As a TEER 3 occupation, Floor Covering Installers work with a diverse range of materials including carpet, hardwood, vinyl, and linoleum. In 2026, as Canada continues its massive push for residential housing and high-end commercial office retrofits, these workers are classified as Essential Interior Systems Talent. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – General construction tradesMinor Group7311 – Roofers, glaziers, painters, decorators and floor covering installersNOC Code73113

duties: Surface Inspection: Inspect, measure, and mark surfaces to be covered to ensure they are level and free of contaminants.
Blueprint Reading: Read blueprints, drawings, and specifications to determine work requirements and material quantities.
Underlay Installation: Measure, cut, and fasten underlay and underpadding to provide a stable base for the final flooring.
Carpet Installation: Measure, cut, and install carpeting using hand or machine stitchers, seaming irons, and bonding tape.
Stretching & Securing: Stretch carpeting using knee-kickers or power stretchers and secure it to the floor using staple guns or tack strips.
Hardwood Flooring: Prepare and install hardwood floors, such as strip, block, or plank floors, using glue, staples, nails, or click-lock systems.
Resilient Flooring: Measure, cut, and install resilient floor coverings (vinyl, linoleum) using specialized adhesives, rollers, and hand tools.
Finishing Touches: Install floor transitions, baseboards, and molding to ensure a seamless and professional finish.
Repair & Restoration: Inspect and repair damaged floor coverings, including patching carpet or replacing individual hardwood planks.
Cost Estimation: Provide professional estimates for material and labor costs to residential and commercial clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a two-to-three-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, over four years of work experience in the trade and some industry-certified courses may be accepted.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and to maximize points in the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP).
Physical Stamina: Ability to bend, kneel, and lift heavy materials for extended periods is essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, over 66% of workers in this NOC receive at least one type of non-wage benefit. Experienced self-employed installers in major cities often earn annual revenues between $75,000 and $110,000.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. While automation impacts manufacturing, the physical installation of flooring remains a high-touch human skill. Projections indicate that the sector will face a steady wave of retirements, and as Canada accelerates the construction of high-density housing, the demand for resilient and easy-to-maintain flooring installers will remain robust through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Floor Covering Installers in 2026 is Stable to Moderate, with high concentrations in urban growth hubs like Toronto, Calgary, and Vancouver. While the general construction market is balanced, there is a specific labor shortage for installers specialized in sustainable hardwood and luxury vinyl plank (LVP) systems. Approximately 80% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, though a significant 54% are self-employed, offering newcomers a rapid path to entrepreneurship in the Canadian market.
The "Sustainable Floor" Boom: Gain experience with eco-friendly bamboo, recycled rubber, and low-VOC adhesive systems required for LEED-certified buildings.
Entrepreneurial Freedom: This trade has one of the highest rates of self-employment in Canada, making it ideal for those looking to start their own contracting business.
Red Seal Global Standard: Earning a Red Seal allows you to work anywhere in Canada and is recognized internationally as a mark of high-skill proficiency.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is frequently included in Category-Based Selection for Express Entry, often allowing candidates to bypass higher CRS cut-offs.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a high priority for 2026 category-based selection draws.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For those with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: A dedicated route for installers with at least one year of experience in Ontario.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those currently employed in the province with a valid job offer from a flooring contractor.
